generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LIDAR sensor mounted on the upper chassis, delivering raw point‐cloud data for obstacle detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloud
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects vehicle speed, battery level, motor status, and steering status via on‑board GPS/INS and IMU for telemetry."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleTelemetry
]

instance CameraObjectDetectionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Camera sensor providing derived object tracks from on‑board camera streams."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraObjectTracks
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateTelemetrySensor
]

instance CameraObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CameraObjectDetectionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data (Data layer)
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Clustered LiDAR point‑cloud data post‑processing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloud
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance VehicleTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed vehicle telemetry data after unit conversion."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance CameraObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Serialized camera object tracks with unit alignment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CameraObjectTracks
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Serializes, aligns, and performs unit conversions on incoming sensor data following ISO 23247."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteCommandEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Facilitates remote command dispatch from operators to the vehicle actuator controller over WiFi UDP."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "The UI streams real‑time vehicle state data, displays the projected driving path, and overlays detected object tracks within proximity. It also provides data export, report generation, and temporal playback features."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides DrivingPathVisualizationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTracksOverlayInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ExportDataAction
    DTDFVocab:provides GenerateReportAction
    DTDFVocab:provides PlaybackLogAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Hosts the ACC calibration pipeline, offloading sensor‑fusion and speed‑planning computation from the vehicle’s resource‑constrained ECU to remote cloud or edge resources. Computed desired speeds are returned to the vehicle actuator controller."
    DTDFVocab:provides OffloadAccCalibrationAction
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputeDesiredSpeedAction
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputationLatencyInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operators can remotely enable/disable ACC, adjust desired speed setpoints, and issue emergency braking commands via the UI, ensuring low end‑to‑end latency and safety‑critical constraints."
    DTDFVocab:provides ToggleAccAction
    DTDFVocab:provides AdjustDesiredSpeedAction
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakeAction
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteCommandLatencyInsight
]

// C17: Insights
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides real‑time visualization of vehicle state data."
]

instance DrivingPathVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Displays the projected driving path into the UI."
]

instance ObjectTracksOverlayInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Overlays detected object tracks within proximity on the UI."
]

instance ComputationLatencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Reports low end‑to‑end latency of the ACC computation offloading pipeline."
]

instance RemoteCommandLatencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Reports low end‑to‑end latency of remote command dispatch over WiFi UDP."
]

// C17: Actions
instance ExportDataAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Exports collected data from the Digital Twin to external storage."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance GenerateReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates performance and safety reports based on captured telemetry."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PlaybackLogAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Provides temporal playback of logged vehicle data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OffloadAccCalibrationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Runs the ACC calibration pipeline and returns desired speeds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ComputeDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Computes desired speed based on lead vehicle distance."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ToggleAccAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remotely enables or disables Adaptive Cruise Control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AdjustDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts the operator‑set desired speed setpoint."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issues an emergency braking command to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands originate in the Digital Twin services where the Actuation FE packages steering, motor and servo commands (e.g., actuator setpoints expressed in standardized units). These commands are published as ROS 2 messages, serialized by the Data Translation component, and transmitted over the WiFi UDP network to the vehicle’s physical twin interfaces. The Actuation FE on the vehicle side receives these messages and forwards them to the respective actuator drivers. The current document does not specify authentication, validation, conflict resolution or external feedback mechanisms beyond the basic ROS 2 message flow or confirmation of actuator execution."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system requires that all connected devices share a common notion of time with a synchronization accuracy of 100 ms or tighter, as mandated by safety–critical vehicular standards; this clock alignment is essential for computation offloading and coordinated operation across the Digital Twin and its physical twin. This 100 ms accuracy directly informs the maximum allowable latency in time‑sensitive services and sets the temporal granularity for data acquisition, processing, and actuation events."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is architected using the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, partitioned into three entities: the User Entity, the Digital Twin Entity, and the Physical Twin Entity. Each functional element (FE) is mapped to an individual ROS 2 package, containing one or more ROS 2 nodes that encapsulate the FE’s behavior. The User Interface FE is decomposed into a 3D Visualization FE and a Control Panel FE. The Digital Twin Entity hosts data‑management and control mechanisms, including data storage, while the Physical Twin Entity represents the inherent resources of the vehicle (compute, sensors, actuators) and implements general automotive functions such as point‑cloud clustering and tracking. This entity‑based, component‑to‑host mapping allows each FE to be deployed on distinct hardware resources, thereby supporting decentralized execution and aligning with the ISO 23247 structure."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a ROS 2 middleware stack for intra‑service communication, with message topics published and subscribed over the ROS 2 transport. For communication between the Digital Twin platform and the vehicle’s physical twin, it uses WiFi LANs employing the UDP transport protocol. The network topology follows a node‑based publisher‑subscriber pattern inherent to ROS 2, with no explicit mention of higher‑layer routing, bandwidth constraints, latency targets, redundancy or security protocols such as TLS or certificate‑based authentication in the provided document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Complies with ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing (Parts 1–4), particularly Reference Architecture Part 2 which defines an entity‑based reference architecture comprising Data Collection and Device Control, Digital Twin, and User entities made up of Functional Entities. The standard explicitly states that FE implementation is open; an example of FE building blocks is OPC Unified Architecture, indicating compatibility with OPC UA protocols for data acquisition, device control, and interoperability. No additional certification or compliance framework is specified in the document."
]
